title: First Class: Herbology
prose: Greenhouse One is warm and damp after the dungeons, earthy-smelling, rows of plants rustling for no wind anyone can feel. Professor Sprout meets you all in patched gardening gloves, mud already on one cheek.

"Nothing here bites," she says cheerfully, "not in this greenhouse, anyway," which is not, on reflection, entirely reassuring. Today's lesson is repotting a tray of small, wriggling bulbs that squeal faintly whenever they're disturbed.
By the end of the hour your fingernails are black with soil and your ears are ringing slightly, but the tray of newly-potted plants looks, genuinely, like something you made.

choices.label: Take your time and handle each bulb carefully.
Work quickly to get through the whole tray.
